entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) used in relay and intercrop systems

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Vigna unguiculata) used in relay and intercrop systems

entities.name: RELAY CROPPING
entities.description: A cropping system where a second crop is planted into a standing first crop to exploit a long growing season

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same field

entities.name: SAHELIAN ZONE
entities.description: The semi-arid region of Africa where the study was conducted

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the field trials were conducted

entities.name: EARLY ONSET OF RAINS
entities.description: Rainy season starting 15-20 days earlier than average, extending the growing season

entities.name: GROWING SEASON
entities.description: The period of time during which a crop grows, ranging from 139 to 150 days in this study

entities.name: DRY MATTER
entities.description: The mass of the plant excluding water

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the leaves of a plant

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area

entities.name: COMPETITIVE EFFECTS
entities.description: Negative interactions between crops competing for resources in intercropping systems

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: A group of grass crops grown for their edible seeds

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of plants that produce seeds in pods
